comments.text: Dark skies reflect my sadness at the firings of all the humans that help keep the #GreatLakes as healthy as possible. Tonight I mourn the loss of the 14 .bsky.social employees that kept the invasive sea lamprey at bay. My fishery will be hurt. 😢😢🌊🌊

The Great Lakes states have, for years, been fighting an invasion of lamprey eels, which were devastating the fish stocks in the lakes. Trump fired the people who've almost succeeded in doing that. www.bridgemi.com/michigan-env...

Sweeping layoffs of federal employees have struck the program responsible for controlling the invasive sea lamprey that threatens fish across the Great Lakes, the earth’s largest freshwater ecosystem.

Yesterday, I saw firsthand how invasive and downright diabolical sea lampreys are — and what will happen to our Great Lakes if we stop work on this invasive species. To demonstrate how they they swarm, the scientists had me spit into a barrel of these suckers — and you can see the results yourself.
I'm a Sucker for Lampreys (And You Should Be Too!), a comic I made about one of my favorite animals in 2024 and printed as a zine. (1/2)
A small government-funded program has reduced the sea lamprey population in the Great Lakes by more than 90 per cent, saving the region’s $6 billion fishing industry. Now the program is at risk.

There are 4 native lamprey species in the #GreatLakes (chestnut, silver, American brook + northern brook) and 1 invasive (sea lamprey). Chestnut lamprey is parasitic like the sea lamprey, but they cause less harm because of their much smaller size + co-evolution with native fish #25DaysofFishmas
The lamprey is a very scary aquatic animal and I’m fully shook to have learned that they live in the Great Lakes. I thought those vampiric fuckers were deep sea dwellers.

While this is good news, the #GreatLakes lamprey control program is now several weeks behind schedule for this year's treatment, and it’s still unclear how many of the previously-fired workers will actually choose to return www.michiganpublic.org/environment-...

We spoke up and fought back. Now critical programs are being reinstated. The Trump Administration this week reversed its freeze on staffing and funding the Great Lakes Sea Lamprey Control Program. When we hold folks accountable, we get results.
With everything going on, it’s good to know that a Senator from Michigan and a Senator from Ohio agree that we must do everything we can to keep the Sea Lamprey out of our Great Lakes.

🎶Chestnuts roasting on an open fire🎶 As another cool native #GreatLakes species often mistaken for being invasive, the Chestnut Lamprey (Ichthyomyzon castaneus) is perfect for today’s #GarWeek theme of “Invasive Species Awareness”! Though they may not be winning any beauty contests #25DaysofFishmas

Okay correct me if I’m wrong, but this BBC Wildlife illustration accompanying an article about unisexual Great Lakes mole salamanders sure looks like a Spotted Sandpiper (Ambystoma maculatum), notably not one of the five (5!!) species’ genomes present in the hybrid unisex Great Lakes populations.

This sucker is a sea lamprey—a parasite that can kill 40 pounds of fish in the Great Lakes in a year. My bill, the Great Lakes Fishery Research Reauthorization Act, would fund research to stop threats like sea lampreys from wreaking havoc on our native species. .bsky.social

It is tough to do sea lamprey control in the US waters of the Great Lakes when front line control agents are fired. Just one of the consequences associated with random terminations of environmental professionals in the US.
www.bridgemi.com/michigan-env...

New research shows that COVID-19 disruptions of lampricide applications has led to increased adult invasive sea lamprey abundance across the #GreatLakes basin. Consistent treatment is critically important + reductions in suppression can quickly lead to sea lamprey resurgence doi.org/10.1093/fshm...

Day 26 of Spooky Science 🧪 The lamprey’s mouth could easily be the cover of a horror film. The lamprey is an eel-shaped fish with a cartilage skeleton, belonging to a primitive group of jawless fishes called Agnathans. Learn more here: www.dec.ny.gov/animals/7242...
keeping this in mind for if any "Lamprey Eels Run Rampant In Great Lakes, Devastating Industry" headlines pop up
A yellow collared scape moth (Cisseps fulvicollis) feeds from goldenrod along the shores of Lake Michigan.

Not much is scarier for a #GreatLakes fish than the invasive sea lamprey, sometimes called vampire fish 🧛 But while they're more trick than treat in their introduced range, they're actually threatened in parts of their native range of the Atlantic coasts of Europe + North America - that's scary!

That will collapse the great lakes fisheries. The invasive sea lamprey (Petromyzon marinus) that was responsible for the collapse during the 1940-1960's is only controlled through joint efforts of both nations and these agreements. What a damned moron.
Sea lamprey for #SundayFishSketch. Learned about potential genetic biocontrol in the #GreatLakes last week.

This week I learned how to tan fish skins into fish leather. I did some lake whitefish and sea lamprey. The yellow skin was dyed with turmeric. It was a very interesting process and something that I can see pursuing as a hobby and as a training offering for fisheries folks! #GreatLakes
what a good thing that no one's threatening the #GreatLakes sea lamprey management program oh no www.bridgemi.com/michigan-env...
Good news for Great Lakes lovers. GLFC plans to keep sea lamprey program on track despite staff uncertainties #906Life #mymichigan www.uppermichiganssource.com/2025/03/26/r...

It’s time to do AWAY with the LAMPREYS. They’re killing our fish and decimating our ecosystems. That’s why we’re celebrating $2.1 million in grant dollars secured for the Great Lakes Fishery Commission to control sea lamprey in Lake Michigan–right here in Grand Rapids.

Wiping out the entire USFWS invasive lamprey team in Michigan is going to end up ruining the multi-billion dollar Great Lakes commercial fishery. 14 people making about 32K a year, we’re single handily keeping the lamprey at bay. And now they’re fired. Trump is an idiot
This is…weird but the Trump administration cut programs that eradicated lampreys from the Great Lakes.

Unlike the Sea Lamprey (Petromyzon marinus) which is invasive in the #GreatLakes, Ohio lamprey have evolved with host fishes in their native range + rarely kill them. Ohio lamprey are also much smaller than sea lamprey (max length = 15 in for Ohio lamprey vs. 47 in for sea lamprey) #25DaysofFishmas

Proper maintenance of our Great Lakes is essential to our economy and environment, which includes controlling invasive species like the sea lamprey. The Trump Admin’s widespread firing of USFWS employees tasked with lamprey control efforts is highly concerning to communities along the Great Lakes.🧵

😭Great Lakes anglers. Rep. Jack Bergman, House DOGE member, didn't blink when Musk fired 14 USFS lamprey killers. Together they cost very little compared to having billions of lampreys grow to collapse the $7 BILLION annual Great Lakes fishing economy. Again. 🐍Time to call Jack: (202) 225-4735
Lake trout were nearly wiped out in Lake Superior by the invasive sea lamprey. But a successful control project has helped bring the trout back from the brink.

UNLOCKED BONUS EPISODE: Flying Specialist Lamprey Killers To The Great Lakes It's the final free episode of Freemium Freebruary! Lucy, Theo, Andrew, and Ben bring you: The very near future of furcons, very nearly hitting it big, striking an unconventional bargain to hit it big & too many lampreys

Our Great Lakes coalition made WAVES. When the Trump Administration and DOGE slashed jobs at USFWS, I joined with Rep. Dingell and called out how dangerous this would be for the Great Lakes as we fight the invasive sea lamprey. It worked.

Here's the weird COVID news for the week. It turns out that populations of the invasive Sea Lamprey in the Great Lakes are rebounding in response to eradication efforts pausing during the pandemic. Populations of their favorite prey, Lake Trout, rebounded in recent years but are now at risk. 🌎🌿🧪

Calling all budding artists! This year's Wildlife Forever Fish Art Contest has a #GreatLakes Fish specialty award. K-12 students are eligible. And who doesn't want the chance to win a model of a sea lamprey?! Submissions are due Feb. 28 #sciart www.theartofconservation.org/great-lakes-...

Surveys of the lamprey show their numbers have returned to pre-pandemic levels, according to a report from the Great Lakes Fishery Commission. Story by .bsky.social:
www.greatlakesnow.org/2026/01/12/i...

Cuts to the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service have slashed the Great Lakes sea lamprey control program. The remaining staff are left scrambling to ensure that populations of the invasive eel don’t skyrocket as a result.

New poster alert! We just released "Lampreys of the Great Lakes" with the Great Lakes Fishery Commission, available now in our store. And now is the perfect time to order with our holiday discount (**get 20% off your order with promo code HOLIDAY20 through December 18**)! buff.ly/PLDvGnK

It's National Invasive Species Awareness Week. Learn more about control efforts for sea lampreys, which now face setbacks as federal staff cuts threaten progress — despite the Great Lakes Fishery Commission’s recent announcement of lake trout’s full rebound: www.greatlakesnow.org/2025/02/lake...

Officials celebrate the near-eradication of invasive creature in Great Lakes: 'This is an unprecedented victory' After more than a century of devastation, officials in the Great Lakes have declared victory over the invasive sea lamprey. www.thecooldown.com/outdoors/sea...
Sea Lamprey control reduction during the COVID-19 pandemic corresponds to rapid increase in Sea Lamprey abundance #GreatLakes 🧪 academic.oup.com/fisheries/ad...
Dear Great Lakes Fishermen: $200m represents ten years of funding to fight the invasive Sea Lamprey saving our Lake Trout and Salmon. But DOGE cut it out and you got this bullshit ad instead.

“I always tell people that if I ever feel like our funding is in doubt I’ll just come with big tanks of sea lamprey and release them in their offices in Congress or in Parliament.” Ethan Baker, chair, Great Lakes Fishery Commission that works to suppress the population of invasive sea lampreys 🧪🌎

Absolutely tickled to see Underground Resistance sharing a booth at a festival with a group educating folks on the importance of lamprey management in the Great Lakes. They had a tank full of live lampreys and they were pulling them out and explaining the biology of lampreys.

Any reduction in sea lamprey control will devastate Great Lakes Fisheries! Hopefully, this move is similar to the "fire the guys who maintain the nukes and then re-hire them" sort of thing...but somehow I doubt Trump cares about fisheries. www.greatlakesnow.org/2025/02/trum...
What's up with Sea Lamprey in the #GreatLakes? I wrote about it in the current issue of Inland Seas!

lol, the administration who fired all the sea lamprey control workers suddenly gives a shit about Asian Carp and the Great Lakes? fuck offfff lol
www.cbc.ca/news/canada/...

Saving the Great Lakes from being destroyed by algae blooms and invasive lampreys requires US-Canada cooperation. The commercial fishery depends on it. This documentary explores the fishers who work those waters. www.cbc.ca/news/canada/...

Reduced Sea Lamprey control efforts during the 2020 Covid shutdown caused an increase in #GreatLakes lamprey numbers. But control efforts since then have already brought them back under control. This demonstrates the importance of maintaining control efforts.
www.michiganpublic.org/environment-...

Oh it's not the lamprey that will be the problem. They also fired the USACE people holding back the asian carp at Chicago. Once that fails all the Great Lakes will suffer a shock way bigger than the lamprey and zebra mussels combined. icrcc.fws.gov
Speaking as a biologist from the Great Lakes where we have lots of invasive sea lamprey, it's a fun party trick to stick them on you (regardless of political party).

The sea lamprey have no natural predators in the Great Lakes. Scientists figured out a chemical that stopped their breeding in the 1950s, but didn’t hurt other species. Now we are stopping the scientists. insideclimatenews.org/news/2702202...
Great Lakes fish biologist here, can confirm - definitely not a lamprey

As someone who grew up on Lake Michigan, I know how important it is to keep the sea lampreys under control. Defunding of the lamprey-control program will impact sport fishing, tourism, and sustinance fishing throughout the entire Great Lakes region.

Last night I hung out with a bunch of Sea lampreys for a spooky science event at the Wisconsin Maritime Museum. Fun out reach and opportunity to stick lampreys to my arm! #GreatLakes fisheries have been impacted by this non-local being but control has reduced their numbers 90%.

My partner and I watched this movie thinking his grandpa, Vernon Applegate, was mentioned. Turns out it’s largely about his work ridding the Great Lakes of sea lamprey. Since Trump fired 14 federal workers involved in the lamprey control program, we’re going to see a lot more lamprey and alewives.

For decades, sea lamprey have sucked their way through the Great Lakes, crashing ecosystems, killing native fish, while costing Great Lakes communities Millions. Last week, educational sea lamprey made their way to the House for a special Great Lakes Task Force event during Capitol Hill Oceans Week.
Great Lakes fish at risk as sea lamprey control faces layoffs
Hydraulic characteristics of effective sea lamprey barriers #GreatLakes www.sciencedirect.com/science/arti...
